content: read reviews/94.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/94.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/94.md.

    27Ôćĺ- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link:
    28Ôćĺ  `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/94](https://www.erdosproblems.com/94)`
    29Ôćĺ- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (convex-position points, distance multiplicities, sum-of-squares bound O(n┬│)).
    30Ôćĺ- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[LeTh95] Lefmann, H. and Thiele, T.`
    31Ôćĺ
    32Ôćĺ## 4. Namespace
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos94 ... end Erdos94`.
    35Ôćĺ- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos94`.
    36Ôćĺ- [x] No `open` / `open scoped` statements are present (none needed ÔÇö no Mathlib namespaces are opened).
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ## 5. Custom Definitions
    39Ôćĺ
    40ÔćĺTwo definitions: `ConvexPosition` and `distSqSum`.
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ- [x] Both have LaTeX docstrings (`/-- ... -/`).
    43Ôćĺ- [x] `distSqSum` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `Set.ncard` over a classical set comprehension).
    44Ôćĺ- [x] `ConvexPosition` is not marked `noncomputable` ÔÇö correct, as it returns `Prop` (erased at compile time, no computability issue).
    45Ôćĺ- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    46Ôćĺ- [x] Prop-valued definition uses `UpperCamelCase`: `ConvexPosition`.
    47Ôćĺ- [x] Value-returning definition uses `lowerCamelCase`: `distSqSum`.
    48Ôćĺ- [x] No local notation is introduced (none needed).
    49Ôćĺ- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for the new definitions. *Optional but recommended ÔÇö e.g., a quick test that `ConvexPosition` holds for a triangle.*
    50Ôćĺ
    51Ôćĺ## 6. Theorem Statements
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    54Ôćĺ- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_94` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
    55Ôćĺ- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
    56Ôćĺ- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]`.
    57Ôćĺ- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    58Ôćĺ- [x] Docstring references the source paper: `[LeTh95]`.

    92Ôćĺ

   100Ôćĺ- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

file.content:
# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist ÔÇö `deepmind/94.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

Sources: [`formal-conjectures/README.md`](../formal-conjectures/README.md) (Style Guidelines ┬ž1ÔÇô7),
[`AGENTS.md`](../formal-conjectures/AGENTS.md), and the five custom linters in
`FormalConjectures/Util/`.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line):
  `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 94`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link:
  `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/94](https://www.erdosproblems.com/94)`
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (convex-position points, distance multiplicities, sum-of-squares bound O(n┬│)).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[LeTh95] Lefmann, H. and Thiele, T.`

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos94 ... end Erdos94`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos94`.
- [x] No `open` / `open scoped` statements are present (none needed ÔÇö no Mathlib namespaces are opened).

## 5. Custom Definitions

Two definitions: `ConvexPosition` and `distSqSum`.

- [x] Both have LaTeX docstrings (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `distSqSum` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `Set.ncard` over a classical set comprehension).
- [x] `ConvexPosition` is not marked `noncomputable` ÔÇö correct, as it returns `Prop` (erased at compile time, no computability issue).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Prop-valued definition uses `UpperCamelCase`: `ConvexPosition`.
- [x] Value-returning definition uses `lowerCamelCase`: `distSqSum`.
- [x] No local notation is introduced (none needed).
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for the new definitions. *Optional but recommended ÔÇö e.g., a quick test that `ConvexPosition` holds for a triangle.*

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_94` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions: `snake_case` for terms of Props.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5 52]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references the source paper: `[LeTh95]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem at erdosproblems.com/94 is phrased as a **direct assertion**: "Suppose n points ÔÇŽ Then ÔłĹ f(u_i)┬▓ Ôë¬ n┬│." This is **Case 3** ÔÇö no `answer()` wrapper needed.

- [x] The theorem type is a direct `Prop` (existential statement with the bound) ÔÇö correct for a direct assertion.
- [x] No `answer()` wrapper present ÔÇö correct for Case 3.
- [x] No quantifiers pulled into theorem parameters that should be inside `answer()` (N/A since no `answer()` used).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` ÔÇö valid category. The problem was proved by Lefmann and Thiele (1995), so `research solved` is appropriate.
- [x] Category is not `research open`, so the `sorry` in the proof body is expected (not yet formally verified).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present: `AMS 5 52`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two listed).
- [x] Numbers are in ascending order: 5, 52.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both are valid AMS MSC2020 codes: `5` (combinatorics), `52` (convex and discrete geometry).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line form with `sorry` ÔÇö acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] No variants present. The problem is a single assertion, so none are needed. N/A.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ÔäŁ`, `ÔäĽ`, `ÔłÇ`, `Ôłâ`, `Ôłł`, `Ôłë`, `ÔëĄ`, `Ôëá`, `Ôłž`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] All lines under 100 characters.
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run in this review ÔÇö must be verified in CI.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass ÔÇö must be verified in CI:
  - `linter.style.copyright`
  - `linter.style.category_attribute`
  - `linter.style.ams_attribute`
  - `linter.style.answer_attribute`
  - `linter.style.namespace`

---

## Summary

**Status: PASS (pending build verification)**

The file is clean and fully conformant with the style guidelines. The formalization correctly models the problem as a direct assertion (Case 3, no `answer()` needed), uses appropriate AMS codes (combinatorics + convex geometry), and has well-documented custom definitions. The only unchecked items are:

1. **┬ž5 (optional):** No `@[category test]` sanity-check declarations for the custom definitions `ConvexPosition` and `distSqSum`. This is recommended but not required.
2. **┬ž12:** Build verification not performed ÔÇö must pass in CI.

content.text: The review status is **PASS (pending build verification)**. The only unchecked items are optional (sanity-check tests for definitions) and CI-dependent (build verification). No changes needed to `deepmind/94.lean`.
